verified.ambiguityResolved: Custom Center is UNAMBIGUOUSLY layout-6. layout-5 is 'Three Column' (a separate, out-of-scope widget). The docs' guess of layout-6 is correct.

architectureNotes: Custom Center is a LAYOUT (widget name 'Custom Center', dataType 'layout-6'), NOT an assessment widget. 'layout-6' is absent from support/module-setup.ts ASSESSMENT_DATA_TYPES, so resolveDropTarget routes it to the canvas — it drops onto the Instructions-for-Students page (#desktop_view) via cy.dropComponent('Custom Center'), not into a Generic Step column.

STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT from layout-1..4: layout-6 has TWO .column.one-half columns, but each holds a .question-container-box with .question-container slots driven by fieldData.settings.questionBank (left) / questionBank2 (right). Drop zones are .column-content.first-box (left, onDropComplete boxNo=0) and .column-content.second-box (right, onDropComplete boxNo=1).
Clicking the .sd-item opens #layout-template-setting (.layout-setting-panel) with a Full Width checkbox (.layout-width-setup). Full Width sets data-pagemargin='fullwidth' on the layout .sd-item.
Nested child-drop business rules (one element/widget per column; no layout-inside-layout) are enforced in layout-directive.js at runtime and are NOT DOM-assertable here — deferred.
Preview/Reader/Publish, media/tables/math/special-languages, multipart, persistence-after-reload, refresh and CLP outline/separator display are deferred.
notYetLiveVerified: Selectors are source-derived from layout6.html + layout-setting-panel.html + layout-directive.js but NOT yet run against a live authoring instance. A single headed run will confirm the drop + settings-panel selectors.
